name: Asparagus & Chicken in Black Bean Sauce
recipeInstructions:
Marinating: With a sharp paring knife, scrape chicken meat from thighs; slice into thin strips across the grain. (Breast meat is not preferred for this dish, as the meat is too dry and spongy.) Combine sherry, soy sauce, cornstarch, and sugar in a bowl; massage the liquid into the meat with your fingers. Marinate for 15 to 30 minutes.
Preparation: Wash asparagus and peel the tough white outer skin off the ends. Slice into diagonal sections about 2 1/2 inches long. Rinse fermented black beans. In a bowl, combine and mash black beans, garlic, brown sugar, black soy sauce, and chicken stock; stir. Let stand for 15 minutes.
Stir-Frying: Add 2/3 of the oil to a hot wok. When the oil is very hot, add the chicken. Stir-fry for about 3 minutes on high heat, or until the chicken begins to shrink and firm up. Remove the chicken to a holding bowl. Reheat the wok to high, then add the remaining oil. When the oil is hot, add the black bean sauce. Stir-fry for 1 minute. Add asparagus; mix with the sauce. Stir the sauce with the asparagus until the sauce boils and the cooked chicken is added, tossing to combine. If needed, dribble in a little cornstarch paste; the cornstarch in the chicken marinade might be enough. Toss ingredients until very little liquid remains and is reduced to a glaze. The dish is ready when the asparagus brightens. If there is still too much liquid, remove the ingredients, continue to reduce the sauce, then return the ingredients to coat them with the sauce. Serve in individual portions.
